title: 1. Introduction to agent conversation memory paradigms

blocks.content: LLMs are inherently stateless. Each request must pass the historical message context. Managing this context limits token overhead.

items.title: Full History
items.content: Sends every message. Accurate but runs out of context tokens quickly.

items.title: Sliding Window
items.content: Only keeps the last N messages, discarding older context.

items.title: Summarization
items.content: Summarizes older history into a single paragraph to save tokens.

blocks.content:
Question 1: What is the primary drawback of sending full conversation history with every message?
- A) It is slow to parse on the client.
- B) It exhausts token limits, increasing costs and response latencies.
- C) It requires database write locks.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Full history consumes tokens rapidly.
